items.rationale: Views stablecoins as crypto's proven 'killer app' and stablecoin payments as the path to onboarding billions onchain, hitting a tipping point where efficiency-vs-reach tradeoff flips and network effects kick in.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.
Stablecoin payments could be the answer to how we bring billions of people onchain.

items.rationale: Considers it an underrated category that uniquely lets networks recruit contributors with incentives exogenous to the protocol (e.g., ESPN resolving betting markets); now convinced it is critical to onboarding a billion people.

citations.quote: zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto. Here are some intriguing real world use cases
I'm now convinced that web proofs/zkTLS will play a critical role in getting us there. It's all about incentives.

items.rationale: Sees AI causing a >1,000x explosion in published content that existing infra can't handle; blockchains can provide an open global coordination layer for IP, plus proof-of-authenticity protocols and decentralized incentivized curators.

citations.quote: We need an open, global coordination layer in order for AI generated content to thrive. And this is exactly what blockchains can provide.
We desperately need an open, global coordination layer for IP, and I couldn't be more excited about what Story is building!

items.rationale: Powerful tool for surfacing real-time information and a better 'truth' than polls; frustrated that US restrictions keep the most-informed participants out and degrade accuracy.

citations.quote: Prediction markets have proven to be a powerful tool for surfacing real time information.
If people in the U.S. could participate in election markets, the outputs would be more accurate.

items.rationale: Treats new SNARK designs as a new paradigm and a significant contribution toward the 'endgame solution' for blockchain scalability, with fully open-source implementations.
items.age_note: Peak enthusiasm in Apr 2024 (Jolt); ~5 months old by snapshot but consistent with a long-held theme since Aug 2023.

citations.quote: This is a significant contribution toward the endgame solution for blockchain scalability, and of course the implementation is fully open source
Lasso and Jolt represent a new paradigm in SNARK design.

items.rationale: Tracks adoption via the State of Crypto Index; encouraged that mobile wallet users, active addresses, and DEX volume hit all-time highs, signaling UX improvements are bringing mainstream users in with 20x+ headroom to grow.

citations.quote: The number of mobile wallet users in crypto is now at an all time high of 28 million
mobile wallet users have now grown month-over-month for 5 consecutive months. March was a breakout month with 27M active mobile wallet users

items.rationale: Early-stage but excited about founders using blockchains to solve some of the hardest and most important problems in the world.

citations.quote: It's early days for DeSci, but I'm grateful for entrepreneurs ... who are using blockchains to solve some of the hardest ... problems in the world!

items.rationale: Believes blockchain coordination can unlock idle physical resources (GPUs, storage, telecom) and democratize access, lowering costs for consumers.
items.age_note: Stated in the Apr 2024 broad-areas thread; part of a list rather than a singled-out recent conviction.

citations.quote: Using blockchain networks to coordinate a broader set of participants, we can unlock additional resources and democratize access to them.

items.rationale: Sees open social protocols as resilient alternatives that can't deplatform users or arbitrarily change rules, letting users own and port their data.
items.age_note: Core conviction restated Apr 2024, but standalone enthusiasm peaked in 2023; not a primary recent theme.

citations.quote: A huge milestone crossed on the path toward more resilient, decentralized social networks for the world.
Decentralized social networks cannot unilaterally make decisions or change their rules for users or developers.

items.rationale: Worries the US is losing its web3 lead and that critical infrastructure could move offshore; frames keeping crypto onshore as a national security issue analogous to semiconductors.
items.age_note: Strongest statements Mar–May 2024; recurring theme since Nov 2023, not restated in the final weeks before snapshot.

citations.quote: Keeping crypto onshore is a matter of national security. Will the U.S. learn from past experiences like semiconductor manufacturing?
there are signs that the US may be losing its lead in web3.

items.rationale: Argues closing the owner-to-active-user gap matters more than expanding the total number of investors; many holders never actually transact on-chain.

citations.quote: Let's close the gap between crypto owners and active on-chain users. This is more important ... than expanding the total number of crypto investors.

items.rationale: Flags risk that embedding wallets too deeply into the app stack abstracts blockchains away from users in a way misaligned with the web3 vision; raised as a debate prompt rather than firm position.
items.age_note: Framed as an open question soliciting both sides, not a settled personal stance.

citations.quote: There is a real risk of over-abstracting wallets and blockchains from users. Embedding wallets too deeply into the application stack is misaligned with ... web3.

items.rationale: Concerned that abundant AI-generated content and deep fakes create a need to track provenance and curate; black-box algorithms controlled by a few large companies 'won't cut it.'

citations.quote: Black box algorithms controlled by a few large companies won't cut it.
it also creates a world of abundant AI-generated content, including deep fakes.

items.rationale: Sees web traffic, market cap, and AI compute consolidating into a handful of firms; views decentralized networks as a needed counterbalancing force.
items.age_note: Articulated most fully in 2023; reinforced via the Apr 2024 areas thread but not a fresh standalone statement.

citations.quote: 3 companies now generate a third of all global web traffic. 5 companies represent 50% of the Nasdaq 100's market cap.

items.rationale: Repeatedly cited as a leading chain on adoption metrics — top chain by monthly active addresses and, alongside Base, capturing the majority of record DEX volume thanks to low fees.
items.age_note: Praise concentrated in the Apr 2024 metrics thread; not restated near snapshot.

citations.quote: In March there were 37M active addresses across all tracked blockchains ... The top chain by monthly active addresses: @solana.
low-transaction fee blockchains like @base and @solana captured a majority of this activity.

items.rationale: Highlighted alongside Solana as a low-fee chain capturing a majority of record decentralized-exchange volume in March 2024.
items.age_note: Single Apr 2024 mention tied to DEX volume data.

citations.quote: low-transaction fee blockchains like @base and @solana captured a majority of this activity.

items.rationale: Framed staking as securing a ~$400B economy of ETH, ERC20s, and NFTs — value beyond yield; treated as foundational infrastructure rather than a fresh trade.
items.age_note: Stated in 2023 (staking/Shanghai era) and never restated; old but never reversed.

citations.quote: On Ethereum, it's used to secure a $400 billion economy of ETH, ERC20 tokens, and NFTs.

risk_regime.rationale: Persistently bullish on adoption and technology: cites the State of Crypto Index and underlying metrics (active addresses, mobile wallets, DEX volume) hitting all-time highs, identifies multiple emerging 'killer apps' (stablecoins, web proofs, crypto x AI), and frames a path to onboarding billions. Notably this is tech/adoption optimism, not price speculation — he explicitly separates speculation from crypto's core value. Concerns (US policy, wallet over-abstraction) are framed as solvable headwinds, not reasons to retreat.

notes: Daren is a data/research-oriented a16z crypto team member; his views skew toward adoption metrics and infrastructure theses rather than token trades, so token-level signals are sparse and inferred from chain-adoption commentary (Solana, Base, ETH). Most-recent signals (Aug–Sep 2024) center on stablecoins as the killer app, web proofs/zkTLS, and crypto x AI content/IP coordination — these are weighted highest. He consistently frames speculation as a byproduct separate from crypto's core value (2024-03-21), so 'risk_on' reflects conviction in technology adoption, not market-price direction. Several 2023/early-2024 theses (decentralized social, DePIN, ZK scalability, Big Tech concentration) persist as standing convictions but were not his freshest emphasis. No genuine reversals were observed in the corpus.
